**SGSC-AI Intelligence Probe Executed**

Nanjing Yike Baitong Education Technology Co., Ltd. provides an extreme contrast specimen of an 'eye-care lamp/school desk sales pitch' erroneously elevated to 'electrical machinery manufacturing' by the commercial registry system. The system has ruthlessly shattered the underlying illusion of a globalized OEM factory brought by the Apple Inc. template. The official database, relying merely on 'lighting fixture sales' and 'plastic products' in its business license, branded it with the heavy-industry mark of 'Electrical Machinery and Equipment Manufacturing.' However, the forensic probe directly touched its rather pathetic physical baseline: this company's paid-in capital is a mere 46,000 RMB, the entire company has only 4 insured employees in 2024, and its headquarters is set up in a regular commercial office on Guanghua East Street (Room 201, Building 11). Bringing 40,000 RMB and 4 people to open a manufacturing factory? This is an insult to industrial common sense. Looking directly at its name 'Education Technology' and its former name 'Jienen Tong Education Technology' (Jienen Tong is a well-known school health lighting brand in China), combined with 'specialized teaching instrument sales,' 'stationery retail,' and 'furniture sales and installation' in its business scope, the truth is glaringly obvious: this is a micro educational equipment proxy (or contractor team) specifically pitching 'eye-care blackboard lights, classroom desks and chairs, and teaching instruments' to schools and educational training institutions. It is only responsible for transporting pre-made fluorescent tubes and desks from someone else's factory to schools to drive screws and install them, unable to manufacture even a single filament for a lightbulb.
